summary: Frontend-only portfolio site fully tested at https://nibras-works.preview.emergentagent.com on desktop (1440x900) and mobile (390x844). All 19 functional checkpoints from the review request passed: page loads without errors, loading screen dismisses, hero shows correct name/tagline/photo/CTAs, CV button is correctly hidden (cv:null), navbar shows 5 sections (Experience hidden because experiences[] is empty), smooth scroll nav works, mobile hamburger menu opens/closes and navigates correctly, About/Skills sections render (4 categories), Experience section not present in DOM, Certificates section renders 1 card with correct title/issuer/'Valid until' badge and credential link, certificate PDF returns HTTP 200 with application/pdf, GitHub Projects section live-fetches and renders 2 cards (DPM-UB-2026, Barattimur-Kopi) with language/star/fork data, Contact section has email + GitHub + LinkedIn + Instagram links, Footer shows '© 2026 Nibras Shiddiq Abyan', scroll progress bar present, all data-testid attributes present. No console/page errors observed.
critical_code_review_comments: useGitHubRepos.js: filter `r => !r.fork` no longer requires a description (comment in file says 'filters for ones with a description' but code only filters forks). Behavior is correct for the current GitHub state where descriptions are missing, but the comment is stale and could mislead future maintainers.
Projects.jsx renders 6 skeleton cards while loading even though only 2 repos will appear; consider matching skeleton count to expected/cached count to reduce layout shift.
Hero.jsx uses `whitespace-nowrap` on the second name line — on very narrow screens this can overflow horizontally. Tested at 390px and it fits, but ultra-narrow viewports may clip.

useGitHubRepos has no rate-limit handling messaging (60 req/hr unauth from GitHub). On rate-limit the user just sees the generic 'Couldn't load repos' message — acceptable but could surface the GitHub link more prominently.
Certificates.jsx 'Valid until' date parsing is fragile (depends on comma+year format like 'December 17, 2028'); any deviation in content.js would render a malformed badge.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Site is a static React+Vite portfolio with no backend. GitHub fetch is live to api.github.com/users/nibrasabyan/repos and currently returns 2 repos (DPM-UB-2026 + Barattimur-Kopi). Both repos lack descriptions so the italic fallback text is shown — this is expected. Use viewport 1440x900 desktop, 390x844 mobile. Set document.documentElement.style.scrollBehavior='auto' for fast nav-click tests. PDF at /certificate-idea-ai.pdf and photo at /photo.jpg both serve 200 from public/. No auth, no env credentials needed.
